Agent autonomously harvesting data through tool chains beyond authorised scope

Tool misuse impact assessed — what harm is possible if each tool is misused autonomously
Tool and MCP server providers assessed as third-party AI components — security obligations in contracts
Agent tool access managed as privileged access — minimum scope, regular review
Formal process for granting agent tool access — documented justification per tool
All tool invocations logged — tool identity, parameters, agent identity, timestamp
Tool parameter validation as secure development requirement

mappings.control_name: Verify access control enforces least privilege
mappings.notes: Agent tool access scoped to minimum required operations — read-only by default, write access formally approved

mappings.control_name: Verify all sensitive functions have access control
mappings.notes: All destructive tool operations require explicit authorisation — not inheritable from agent session

mappings.notes: All tool invocations logged — tool identity, parameters, agent session, timestamp

mappings.notes: Tool chain exploitation scenarios identified in threat model — mitigations implemented and verified
Tool API endpoints rate-limited — anomalous invocation frequency detected
Per-tool minimum permission — setpoint adjustment tool restricted to specific tags and safe ranges
Allowlisted tool operations for each agent role — agent cannot invoke tools outside its defined function
Anomalous tool invocation patterns detected and responded to — alert, suspend, investigate
Tool descriptor integrity verified — poisoned MCP tool descriptors rejected at loading
Excessive privilege is specifically listed as OT vulnerability class
Assess agent permission scope as part of OT risk register
Least privilege must be enforced at zone boundary for all automated systems
Access permissions managed — per-tool permission manifests, least privilege per tool, irreversibility classification
Policy defines permissible tool invocations — which tools require human confirmation
All tool invocations logged and monitored — anomalous parameters, unusual sequences, high frequency detected
Root cause analysis — which tool was misused, what downstream impact occurred, what parameters were used

mappings.control_id: Logical access controls enforce tool permissions — agent cannot invoke tools outside authorised scope

mappings.control_id: Monitoring for anomalous tool invocations — destructive parameters, out-of-scope tools, unexpected sequences detected

mappings.control_id: Tool invocations are complete and accurate — parameter validation ensures tool calls match intended business operation
mappings.notes: Validation configuration, rejected call logs

mappings.control_id: Agent tool permissions follow least-privilege — agent can only access CHD systems required for defined function

mappings.control_id: Tool parameter validation in agent code — LLM-generated tool parameters validated before execution

All tool integrations assessed as supply chain components — integrity verification, vendor security review before integration
All agent tool invocations logged — data volumes, destination, and parameter patterns monitored for anomalies

Formalise agent identity and permission lifecycle
Periodic review of declared vs granted permissions per agent
Alert when agent requests permissions outside declared scope

mappings.control_id: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
mappings.notes: Analogy: agent accepts and executes tool payloads without content validation

mappings.control_id: Improper Control of Dynamically-Managed Code Resources
mappings.notes: MCP descriptors and tool specifications are dynamically loaded code resources — modification not controlled
Tool parameters generated by LLM not validated against safe ranges before execution
Attempt to invoke tools outside the agent's defined role; test parameter ranges; verify irreversibility controls
Verify each tool enforces its own access controls independently of the agent framework
Verify tool call parameters generated by the LLM are validated before execution

mappings.control_name: Enforce user access privileges to AI systems

mappings.control_id: Agent with excess privilege can call tools in destructive ways even within normal use
mappings.notes: Per-tool permission manifests — each tool scoped to minimum required operations

mappings.control_id: Compromised third-party tool identity gets full scope of agent credential
mappings.notes: Validate all third-party tool identities — reject tokens from unregistered issuers

mappings.control_id: Single shared credential allows tool misuse to affect multiple services
mappings.notes: Unique credential per tool integration — tool-scoped tokens, not agent-wide tokens

mappings.control_id: Define explicit security requirements specifying the maximum permitted tool access, API scope, data source access, and resource boundaries for each agent deployment
mappings.notes: Establishes access control as a mandatory deployment requirement

mappings.control_id: Threat model all agent access paths to tools, data stores, and APIs; design least-privilege tool manifests and enforce tenant isolation by design
mappings.control_name: PW.2.1-PS – Design software to meet security requirements
mappings.notes: Ensures access boundaries are designed before implementation

mappings.control_id: Protect agent configuration files, tool manifests, permission policies, and orchestration definitions from unauthorised modification
mappings.control_name: PS.1.1-PS – Protect all code from unauthorised access
mappings.notes: Prevents tampering with access control configuration

mappings.control_id: Review agent access control enforcement — verify that tool permission manifests, RBAC policies, and tenant isolation boundaries are correctly implemented and cannot be bypassed
mappings.control_name: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
mappings.notes: Validates access controls before production deployment
Enforce role-based access control on all agent resources — tools, data stores, APIs, and inter-agent communication channels; deny by default
Enforce least privilege for all agent permissions; restrict tool access, data store access, and API scope to minimum necessary per agent role
Restrict agents to minimum necessary capabilities; disable unused tools, APIs, and action types; enforce capability restrictions in agent configuration
Log all agent access decisions — permitted and denied — with sufficient detail for access control review and incident investigation

mappings.control_name: Protection and Prevention — agent access controls
mappings.notes: Implement security controls enforcing least privilege on agent tool access, data store access, and API permissions within financial systems

mappings.control_name: ICT Risk Management — agent permission governance
mappings.notes: Include agent access control policies in ICT risk management framework; define acceptable permission scopes per agent role and financial function
Monitor agent access patterns for unauthorised tool invocations, data access beyond scope, and permission violations; alert on detection
Include agent access control bypass in resilience testing; verify that agents cannot exceed defined permission boundaries under adversarial conditions
